name: union-contract
topic: union negotiating a new contract with management

rubrics: Facts should cover the bargaining structure and the information asymmetry between the parties.
Facts should include fallback positions or strike leverage without assuming a specific industry.
Facts should avoid inventing facts that are not grounded in the prompt.
2-3 players: union, management, optionally a mediator or government regulator with genuine power.
Each player must have has-objective relationship. Objectives must be specific: wage targets, cost control, job security.
Player entities must include playerType and knowledge array reflecting information asymmetry.
Should identify a bargaining game with gameType 'bargaining'.
Strategies should include: strike/work stoppage, lockout, compromise, concession, delay.
In a full pipeline run, plays-in and has-strategy relationships should link players to games and strategies.
Must include assumption about relative bargaining power (capability category).
Must include assumption about information asymmetry (information category).
Each assumption must have sensitivity rating and dependencies referencing prior-phase entities.
Must include a negotiated settlement scenario as baseline.
Must include a strike/lockout escalation scenario with tail-risk subtype.
Central thesis must be falsifiable and specific to this negotiation.
Each scenario must specify prediction_basis and reference key_assumptions.
